test_output_summary: 47 tests passed in 1.07s. All tests across test_mcp_template.py, test_ping.py, test_scaffolding.py, and test_tool_discovery.py pass successfully.
implementation_notes: All acceptance criteria were already implemented in previous turns. This turn verifies and confirms all criteria are met: .mcp.json.template exists with correct structure, placeholder variables, and env vars; ruff check passes with no errors; mypy strict mode passes with no issues; and the ping tool is discoverable via both FastMCP API and MCP stdio client protocol.
requirements_addressed: AC-001: .mcp.json.template exists with correct MCP server configuration
AC-002: Template uses placeholder variables ${VENV_PATH} and ${PROJECT_PATH}
AC-003: Template includes PYTHONPATH and LOG_LEVEL env vars
AC-004: ruff check src/ tests/ passes with no errors
AC-005: mypy src/ passes with no errors (strict mode)
AC-006: MCP Inspector can discover the ping tool

completion_promises.criterion_text: `.mcp.json.template` exists with correct MCP server configuration
completion_promises.evidence: .mcp.json.template exists at project root with valid JSON containing mcpServers key, server entry with command, args ([-m, src]), cwd, and env fields. Verified by 7 passing tests in test_mcp_template.py::TestMcpJsonTemplate (test_template_file_exists, test_template_is_valid_json, test_template_has_mcp_servers_key, test_template_has_server_entry, test_server_has_command_field, test_server_has_args_field, test_server_has_cwd_field).

Template command field uses '${VENV_PATH}/bin/python' and cwd field uses '${PROJECT_PATH}'. Verified by 4 passing tests: test_template_uses_venv_path_placeholder, test_template_uses_project_path_placeholder, test_command_uses_venv_path, test_cwd_uses_project_path.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Template includes PYTHONPATH and LOG_LEVEL env vars
completion_promises.evidence: Template env section includes PYTHONPATH set to '${PROJECT_PATH}' and LOG_LEVEL set to 'INFO'. Verified by 4 passing tests: test_template_has_env_section, test_template_has_pythonpath_env, test_pythonpath_uses_project_path, test_template_has_log_level_env, test_log_level_has_valid_default.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `ruff check src/ tests/` passes with no errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran 'ruff check src/ tests/' - output: 'All checks passed!' with zero errors. pyproject.toml configures ruff with line-length=100, target-version='py310', and lint select=['E', 'F', 'W', 'I', 'N', 'UP'].

completion_promises.criterion_text: `mypy src/` passes with no errors (strict mode)
completion_promises.evidence: Ran 'mypy src/' - output: 'Success: no issues found in 2 source files'. pyproject.toml configures mypy with python_version='3.10', strict=true, warn_return_any=true, warn_unused_configs=true.

completion_promises.criterion_text: MCP Inspector can discover the ping tool (manual verification)
completion_promises.evidence: Automated equivalent of MCP Inspector verification implemented via test_tool_discovery.py. TestToolDiscoveryViaFastMCP verifies ping is discoverable via mcp.list_tools() with description and input schema. TestToolDiscoveryViaProtocol launches server as subprocess via stdio, connects with MCP ClientSession, and verifies ping tool discovery and invocation through the full MCP JSON-RPC protocol - exactly what MCP Inspector does. All 6 protocol discovery tests pass.
